Solution (excerpt)
Reference action trace: 1. [link] Tabs -> CLICK 2. [link] Tab -> CLICK 3. [svg] -> CLICK 4. [link] Most popular of all time -> CLICK 5. [link] Stairway To Heaven -> CLICK 6. [button] ADD TO PLAYLIST -> CLICK…
Difficulty dimensions
domain_depth
1/5 No specialized knowledge required; standard web UI navigation.
reasoning_complexity
2/5 Multi-step procedure following a linear sequence with no branching or inference needed.
conceptual_leaps
1/5 Direct application of a known UI procedure; no non-obvious jumps required.
plan_length
3/5 6 distinct UI actions (clicks) to complete the task.
interdependence
5/5 Fully sequential; each click depends on the previous navigation state, and any missed step breaks the flow entirely.
Justification: —
Step graph — 10 nodes | bottlenecks: #3, #5, #6 The solution is a linear UI navigation chain dominated by atomic computational clicks; the critical bottlenecks are the Tabs entry point, the SVG expand action (which reveals the popularity filter), and selecting 'Most popular of all time', as missing any of these prevents reaching the target track.
#1 [strategic] Identify UI workflow to add tab to playlist complexity: 2/5
Determine that the task requires navigating a tabs/browse interface to find the 'Most popular of all time' section and then adding a track to a playlist.
#2 [conceptual] Locate the Tabs navigation section complexity: 2/5 ← #1
Identify that the top-level 'Tabs' link is the entry point to the browsing/tab interface needed to reach the target content.
#3 [computational] Click Tabs link complexity: 1/5 ← #2
Click the top-level 'Tabs' link to open the tabs navigation area.
#4 [computational] Click Tab link complexity: 1/5 ← #3
Click the secondary 'Tab' link within the Tabs section to expand or navigate to the sub-tab listing.
#5 [computational] Click SVG icon to expand options complexity: 2/5 ← #4
Click the SVG icon (likely a dropdown or expand arrow) to reveal additional tab filter options including popularity-based views.
#6 [computational] Click Most popular of all time link complexity: 1/5 ← #5
Click the 'Most popular of all time' link to filter or navigate to the list of most popular tracks of all time.
#7 [conceptual] Identify top track to add to playlist complexity: 1/5 ← #6
Recognize that 'Stairway To Heaven' is the top result in the 'Most popular of all time' list and is the track to be added.
#8 [computational] Click Stairway To Heaven link complexity: 1/5 ← #7
Click the 'Stairway To Heaven' track link to open its detail or action page.
#9 [computational] Click ADD TO PLAYLIST button complexity: 1/5 ← #8
Click the 'ADD TO PLAYLIST' button to add the selected track to the playlist, completing the task.
#10 [verification] Confirm track added to playlist complexity: 1/5 ← #9
Verify that the UI confirms the track was successfully added to the playlist (e.g., success message or playlist update).
#101 WorkArena++ web agent / Music (Mind2Web proxy — Work Trivial (1) nodes: 8 | bottlenecks: 2 m2w_3e0d115f-9da7-4211-9d99-122912890b40 ▼
Problem
Delete the history of what i heard recently.
Solution (excerpt)
Reference action trace: 1. [link] Library -> CLICK 2. [link] History -> CLICK 3. [button] Clear all history -> CLICK 4. [button] Clear my history -> CLICK…
Difficulty dimensions
domain_depth
1/5 No specialized knowledge required; clearing listen history is a standard consumer app pattern.
reasoning_complexity
1/5 The solution is a direct lookup and execution of a known UI procedure with no inference needed.
conceptual_leaps
1/5 No non-obvious jumps; the path Library → History → Clear is a conventional navigation pattern.
plan_length
2/5 Four sequential UI actions: navigate to Library, click History, click Clear all history, confirm.
interdependence
5/5 Steps are fully sequential; each click depends on the previous one having opened the correct UI state.
Justification: —
Step graph — 8 nodes | bottlenecks: #1, #5 The solution is a strictly linear UI navigation chain with no branching; computational click steps dominate, and the critical bottleneck is correctly identifying Library→History as the path to listening history management.
#1 [strategic] Identify UI workflow to clear listening history complexity: 2/5
Determine that clearing recently heard history requires navigating through the Library section of the application.
#2 [conceptual] Locate Library entry point in UI complexity: 1/5 ← #1
Identify the Library link as the correct top-level navigation destination that contains listening history management.
#3 [computational] Click Library link complexity: 1/5 ← #2
Click the Library link to navigate into the library section of the application.
#4 [conceptual] Identify History subsection within Library complexity: 1/5 ← #3
Recognize that the History subsection within Library contains the recently heard content and its management controls.
#5 [computational] Click History link complexity: 1/5 ← #4
Click the History link to open the listening history view.
#6 [computational] Click Clear all history button complexity: 1/5 ← #5
Click the 'Clear all history' button to initiate the deletion process for all listening history entries.
#7 [computational] Confirm by clicking Clear my history complexity: 1/5 ← #6
Click the 'Clear my history' confirmation button to finalize and irreversibly delete the listening history.
#8 [verification] Verify history is cleared complexity: 1/5 ← #7
Confirm that the history list is now empty or that a success message is displayed, indicating the deletion was successful.
